title: eriogonum umbellatum subsp. dumosum
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum subsp. dumosum, also known as buckwheat sage, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Polygonaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of buckwheat sage include its ability to reduce fever, relieve digestive issues, and act as a natural antihistamine, making it a popular remedy for allergies. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its numerous health benefits.
